Duane Morris 1 role · 2 matches · best 160
Business Reorganization and Financial Restructuring Associate (PA/NY/TX/SF)View Job ↗
2026-02-11
New York City, New York; San Francisco, California; Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; Dallas, Texas · Bankruptcy · 3–5 yrs · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Duane Morris LLP seeks an associate to join its Business Reorganization and Financial Restructuring practice group. The ideal candidate should have 3-5 or more years of sophisticated commercial bankruptcy practice including both litigation and transactional experience. Candidate must have an aptitude for client contact, have the willingness to work independently, be a team player, be able to work/thrive in a fast-paced environment, and be detail-oriented. Stellar academics and superior writing and research skills are required. This position is available in our New York, Philadelphia, Dallas or San Francisco office along with requisite bar admission in the jurisdiction of practice. The target annual pay range for this role is $250,000-$295,000. Actual pay will vary depending upon various factors, including relevant experience, skill-set, current business needs and market factors. The compensation range listed is just one component of Duane Morris’ total comp package for employees, which, depending on the position, may also include discretionary bonuses and incentive packages, and firm-sponsored benefit programs.

Asset Management AssociateView Job ↗
2026-02-02
District of Columbia; New York City, New York · Corporate · 2–7 yrs · 7 elite matches
Show job description
We are looking for associates with private equity and hedge fund experience between Classes of 2024 and 2019 to join our Asset Management team in DC or NY. The Firm pays market salaries to our attorneys. As such, below is the current associate base salary scale: * 1st year: $225,000 * 2nd year: $235,000 * 3rd year: $260,000 * 4th year: $310,000 * 5th year: $365,000 * 6th year: $390,000 * 7th year: $420,000 * 8th year: $435,000 The actual salary offered will be based on a number of factors including but not limited to the qualifications of the applicant, years of relevant experience, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and if applicable, the location in which the applicant lives and/or from which they will be performing the job. Salary Range $310,000—$435,000 USD

Greenberg Traurig 1 role · 5 matches · best 192
Capital Markets Associate (Mid to Senior Level)View Job ↗
2026-02-09
New York City, New York · Corporate · 5–7 yrs · 5 elite matches
Show job description
Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for a Mid-Level Associate in the Capital Markets Group in New York. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Capital Markets Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. Job Requirements: Ideal candidates will have 5 - 7 years of experience, from a peer BigLaw firm, in public and private equity and debt offerings, including initial public offerings and follow-on offerings, proxy statements and S-4 registration statements in connection with business combination transactions, interfacing with the SEC and other regulators, and familiarity with the ongoing reporting requirements under the ’34 Act for public companies, including 10-Ks, 10-Qs, 8-Ks and proxy statements. Experience in representing both issuers and underwriters would be beneficial. Candidates also should have day-to-day experience counseling public companies, including with respect to corporate governance matters. Candidates should possess a strong academic background, client management skills and drafting and negotiation skills. Candidates must have the skills to retain and grow existing clients and relationships. Candidates also should be admitted to the New York bar. The expected pay range for this position is: $365,000-$420,000. Salary will be determin

Holland & Knight 1 role · 2 matches · best 164
Corporate M&A and Securities Senior AssociateView Job ↗
2026-02-02
Atlanta, Georgia; Austin, Texas; Boston, Massachusetts; Charlotte, North Carolina; Chicago, Illinois; Dallas, Texas; Denver, Colorado; Ft. Lauderdale, Florida; Houston, Texas; Jacksonville, Florida; Tampa, Florida; Los Angeles, California; Miami, Florida; New York City, New York; Orlando, Florida; Portland, Oregon; San Francisco, California; Stamford, Connecticut; Tysons Corner, Virginia; District of Columbia; Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; Orange County, California; Nashville, Tennessee; Seattle, Washington · Corporate · 5–7 yrs · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Job Description: Holland & Knight LLP seeks a senior-level associate to join its dynamic Corporate M&A and Securities Practice. Ideal candidates will have 5-7 years of experience with an emphasis on M&A and private equity transactions. Candidates must have a strong working knowledge of acquisition agreements and the full suite of documentation used in M&A and private equity transactions, as well as proficiency in drafting and negotiating the same. A proven track record supporting private equity sponsors and portfolio companies across the investment lifecycle (initial platform acquisitions, add-ons, portfolio company governance and exits), and familiarity with middle-market deal structures, are strongly preferred. Candidate must have strong interpersonal and communication skills, a high degree of maturity and business sense, and the ability to effectively manage internal and external client relationships. Excellent academic credentials are required and large national or international law firm experience is preferred. Holland & Knight possesses a collegial atmosphere and provides its professionals with a large international platform with tangible opportunities for professional growth and advancement. Job Requirements: 5 to 7 years of experience JD from an ABA Accredited Law School Must be licensed or ready to become licensed to practice law in the Virginia. Norton Rose 2 roles · 2 matches · best 164
Projects Associate (M&A/Dev)View Job ↗
2026-02-06
Austin, Texas; Houston, Texas; Dallas, Texas; Chicago, Illinois; Denver, Colorado; Los Angeles, California; San Francisco, California; New York City, New York; St. Louis, Missouri; District of Columbia · Energy · 3–6 yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
Norton Rose Fulbright is seeking an Associate to join its Projects team. The ideal candidate will have 3 to 6 years of relevant experience and a strong background in domestic and international business transactions and corporate matters, particularly in the energy sector, with location flexibility across any of our US offices. Candidates should have a strong focus on domestic and international business transactions and corporate matters, particularly in the energy sector. This role offers the opportunity to work on high-profile projects involving renewable energy, energy storage, and power generation technologies. Collaborating across our global team, we act on some of the most innovative, complex and challenging projects in the world. Lawyers would have the opportunity to work on 100% renewable energy and energy transition transactions, if desired, or a broader energy and infrastructure practice. This role involves handling a range of M&A transactions, including acquisitions, joint ventures, divestitures of projects, portfolios of projects, and entire development platforms. The role may also include project development work if desired. Our projects practice is one of the largest in the world, with over 350 project and project finance lawyers located across our 50+ offices. We represent lenders, sponsors, developers, institutional investors, contractors, operators, and government entities in major domestic and cross-border renewable energy projects and financings. Show job description
Key Requirements: tailor to specific job 5+ years’ of experience as a projects finance lawyer. Experience drafting and negotiating the principal financing documentation. Experience with renewable energy projects preferred. Experience reviewing project documentation preferred. Strong academic credentials. Location: This position may be based in New York, Chicago, D.C., Los Angeles, and Texas offices. Applicants should indicate their office(s) of interest in their application. The full time base annual salary for this position in the New York City and Los Angeles market is expected to range between $365,000- $435,000. This range represents the firm’s good faith and reasonable estimate of the range of possible compensation at the time of posting for a position in New York City and Los Angeles. Salaries in other markets will vary depending on market data. Bonus opportunities are non-guaranteed and are dependent upon individual and firm performance. Position Information: This is open to Senior Associate and Senior Counsel level candidates. The Senior Counsel position offers a compelling opportunity to build a high‑impact career within a prestigious global law firm, working on sophisticated matters alongside top‑tier partners and clients. Senior Counsel is a partnership‑track role, designed for attorneys seeking long‑term career investment, meaningful responsibility, and significant advancement potential. Mergers & Acquisitions Associate Attorney - MidlevelView Job ↗
2026-02-23
Phoenix, Arizona; Denver, Colorado; Chicago, Illinois; New York City, New York; Dallas, Texas; Seattle, Washington · Corporate · 3–6 yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
Job Description: One of Fortune’s Best Places to Work, Perkins Coie, LLP, a leading international law firm, is seeking Mergers & Acquisitions associates for our Seattle, Denver, Chicago, Dallas, Phoenix and New York offices. We’ve created a firm culture that is based on collaboration, dedication to serving our clients and mutual respect. We are seeking experienced associates to join our expanding Mergers & Acquisitions group. Candidates must have excellent academic credentials and a minimum of three years of experience handling a variety of M&A transactions, including private equity and/or other private target transactions. Experience at an AmLaw 100 firm is required. If you are interested in joining the team, please access the link below to provide your cover letter, resume, law school transcript and deal sheet. This position is eligible for an annual discretionary bonus, 401(k) plan, medical, dental, and vision insurance, personal medical and parental leave, up to ten paid Holidays, and family care benefits. More information regarding benefits and programs may be found here Compensation range: $260,000 to $390,000 annually. Compensation depends on qualifications and experience. #LI-Hybrid

Reed Smith 1 role · 1 match · best 166
San Francisco - Labor & Employment Associate (Mid-to-Senior-Level)View Job ↗
2026-02-20
San Francisco, California · Labor & Employment · 5–? yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
The Labor & Employment practice group is looking to hire an experienced mid-senior level associate to join our San Francisco office. The candidate must have five or more years of experience in employment litigation, including single plaintiff cases and wage and hour class actions. Candidate must also be familiar with Federal Court Rules and E-Discovery. This is a terrific opportunity for an attorney who is interested in being groomed towards partnership to help lead a diverse, supportive team within a friendly office community, has the drive to help build and grow the practice and seeks to work with fun clients on interesting cases. The successful candidate must have excellent writing analytical skills, experience handling multiple cases, and be able to serve as point person on litigation, including supervising junior associates and staff and serving as client contact. Candidate must have demonstrated project management skills and be able to take a case and run with it. Deposition and/or courtroom experience required; employment advice and counseling experience desired. Candidate should possess strong academic credentials from a nationally recognized law school and be licensed to practice in California. Reed Smith values collegiality and diversity among our lawyers and seeks to make the practice both professionally and personally rewarding. We offer a competitive salary and benefits. Reed Smith offers a challenging work environment and a total compensation package that in

Troutman Pepper 2 roles · 2 matches · best 169
Transactional Real Estate Associate - AtlantaView Job ↗
2025-10-29
Atlanta, Georgia · Real Estate · 2–5 yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
Troutman Pepper Locke’s Real Estate practice group is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Associate to join our dynamic team in the Atlanta office. The ideal candidate will have 2 to 5 years of substantive experience in real estate transactional matters, with expertise in real estate debt or equity matters. The successful candidate will handle a variety of real estate transactions, including acquisitions, dispositions, leasing, and development projects, and provide legal advice and support on real estate financing, including debt and equity structuring. Responsibilities include drafting, reviewing, and negotiating complex real estate documents such as purchase agreements, leases, and financing documents, collaborating with clients to understand their business objectives and providing strategic legal guidance, conducting due diligence, and managing closing processes for real estate transactions. The salary range for this position is $235,000 - $310,000, dependent on relevant years of experience. Candidates must be licensed to practice law and in good standing in Georgia. For more information about the practice visit our website here. For inquiries or additional information on this position, please contact Tonja Washington . By applying for this position, you agree and understand that Troutman Pepper Locke will process your Personal Information pursuant to the terms of our Worker and Applicant Global Privacy Notice If you have questions about our data handling practices,

Show job description
Troutman Pepper Locke’s Real Estate practice group is seeking a mid-level associate to be based in the Charlotte, Richmond or Atlanta office with 4 to 6 years of real estate transactional experience, including acquisitions, dispositions, and leasing. The ideal candidate will also have experience with real estate debt or equity matters. They will be confident and comfortable working with clients daily, will have an elevated attention to detail and excellent communication skills. Responsibilities include drafting, reviewing, and negotiating complex real estate documents such as purchase agreements, leases, and financing documents, collaborating with clients to understand their business objectives and providing strategic legal guidance, conducting due diligence, and managing closing processes for real estate transactions. The salary range for this position is $295,000 - $350,000, dependent on relevant years of experience. Candidates must be licensed and in good standing in the state in which they practice. For more information about the practice visit our website here. For inquiries or additional information on this position, please contact Tonja Washington. Winston & Strawn 4 roles · 17 matches · best 193
New York Office - Energy and Infrastructure Transactions AssociateView Job ↗
2026-01-28
New York City, New York · Energy · 3–6 yrs · 3 elite matches
Show job description
Winston & Strawn’s New York office is looking for a mid-level associate who has experience in the energy (particularly power generation and transmission) and infrastructure sectors to be part of highly regarded team. The work focuses on acquisitions and dispositions of and equity investments in projects and portfolios, development of projects, and financings. Experience should include performing and supervising due diligence, drafting of principal and ancillary agreements, coordinating with specialists (tax, environmental, real estate, regulatory, etc.) and managing closings. All candidates should have excellent academic credentials from an accredited law school, as well as strong organizational, analytical, writing, oral communication, and people skills. Candidate should be someone who thrives in a collaborative environment working closely with other lawyers (both partners and associates) and clients. All candidates must be admitted to practice in New York. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. View our associate salary disclosures.

New York Office – Structured Finance AssociateView Job ↗
2026-02-04
New York City, New York · Banking · 4–? yrs · 6 elite matches
Show job description
Winston & Strawn is looking for a mid-level associate to join its structured finance group. Ideal candidates will have at least four years of structured finance experience. We are looking for candidates who have strong deal experience at a law firm with a focus in structured finance, lending, securitization, or similar finance structures. Experience across a variety of asset types and structures is preferred but not required. We are looking for candidates to be placed in our New York office. All candidates must be admitted to the New York State Bar. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. View our associate salary disclosures.

2026-02-11
Charlotte, North Carolina; Chicago, Illinois; Dallas, Texas; Houston, Texas; Los Angeles, California; New York City, New York; San Francisco, California; Silicon Valley, California; District of Columbia; Miami, Florida · Corporate · 4–6 yrs · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Winston & Strawn is looking for a mid-level associate to join their corporate practice group. The ideal candidate will have between four and six years of M&A and private equity experience, specifically in representing financial sponsors and strategic buyers/sellers in connection with all varieties of M&A and private equity transactions, including the preparation of acquisition/disposition agreements and related matters. While Chicago is our preferred location for this position, candidates for our other U.S. offices will be considered. All candidates must be admitted to the state bar of their preferred location. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. View our associate salary disclosures.

Job Description Summary:Fenwick & West has been a leader in technology and life sciences law since 1972. We have offices in Silicon Valley, San Francisco, Santa Monica, Seattle, New York and Washington D.C.. Our nationally recognized patent litigation group represents both patent holders and alleged infringers in federal jurisdictions throughout the United States, as well as in the Patent Office on parallel post-grant proceedings. Job Description: The New York office of Sidley Austin LLP is seeking qualified candidates with at least three years of experience to join its growing Capital Markets practice as associates. Responsibilities include representation of issuers, underwriters and other financial intermediaries in structuring and executing innovative and complex securities offerings and providing disclosure advice. We are seeking associates mid-senior level associates with sponsor or lender side finance experience. The ideal candidate will have experience at a peer firm representing lenders (investment and commercial banks, business development companies or other providers of debt financing) and/or borrowers (including private equity sponsors) in sophisticated financings for acquisitions, buyouts, restructurings, special situations and other corporate finance transactions.
